title: 'The best decision I ever made': How UCLA's roster converged to become NCAA champs
content: How a longtime coach and an eclectic mix of transfers finally delivered an NCAA title years in the making.

title: Luka to seek care in Europe, aims for playoffs
content: Luka Doncic will seek specialized treatment on his Grade 2 left hamstring strain as he and Austin Reaves target a return to the Lakers' lineup during the playoffs.

title: 'We did it!': UCLA wins first women's NCAA title
content: UCLA dominated every phase of the game in Sunday's women's national final, blowing out the Gamecocks for Bruins coach Cori Close's first championship.

title: Staley praises Close: Happy for 'quality people'
content: South Carolina coach Dawn Staley spoke glowingly of UCLA coach Cori Close after the Gamecocks' loss to the Bruins in the NCAA championship game Sunday.

title: Curry returns, puts Warriors 'back in the fight'
content: Warriors star Stephen Curry scored 29 points in 26 minutes Sunday night against the Rockets, a performance that was missing only the final punctuation point.

title: Flagg flips ROY odds after latest 'statement' game
content: After putting up 45 points in a near triple-double Sunday, Cooper Flagg passed Kon Knueppel as the favorite for Rookie of the Year.

title: Sources: Michigan's May not pursuing other jobs
content: Michigan coach Dusty May informed university officials that he's not pursuing any college basketball jobs, sources told ESPN on Sunday.

title: Anthony calls Red Sox's 2-7 start 'unacceptable'
content: The Boston Red Sox fell to a major league-worst 2-7 on Sunday, tying the worst start through nine games in the franchise's long history.

title: Vitello ejected, 'frustration' mounts for 3-7 Giants
content: First-year manager Tony Vitello, 47, was ejected for the first time in his major league career Sunday afternoon, during the San Francisco Giants' 5-2 loss to the New York Mets, his team's third straight defeat and its sixth this season at Oracle Park.

title: 📈 Masters tiers: Ranking favorites, contenders
content: Can Rory McIlroy repeat? Will Scottie Scheffler find more magic at Augusta? We break down the Masters field from the favorites to the players just looking to make the cut.

title: How Michigan, UConn won Saturday's Final Four -- and keys to Monday's title game
content: ESPN's college basketball crew breaks down what they saw in Indianapolis -- and what could decide the championship.

title: NCAA women's basketball Way-Too-Early 2026-27 Top 25 rankings
content: UCLA is the 2026 champion -- but unranked in the first top 25 for next season. Runner-up South Carolina is No. 3.

title: Lauren Coughlin rolls to Aramco win as Nelly Korda second again
content: Lauren Coughlin rolled to a five-shot win over Nelly Korda and the rest of the star-studded field at the Aramco Championship for her first win in two years.

title: 'I've always been a dreamer': Rory McIlroy and the Northern Ireland town that will always be part of his story
content: Through all of McIlroy's up and downs at the Masters, his hometown of Holywood has been right there with him.

content: LA's lead for the 3-seed is down to a half-game. Plus, updated playoff projections, draft lottery standings after Friday.

content: Here's what league insiders are talking about, including when Doncic might return from his latest injury.

content: The biggest (and, in some cases, surprising) picks for the Fire and Tempo had a few things in common.

content: Who are the best quarterbacks in the 2026 class? We sized up the top 12, starting with Fernando Mendoza and Ty Simpson.

content: We have stat projections for the top running back prospects, from Jeremiyah Love to late-round sleepers.

content: The most iconic highlight reel in sports started in a Michigan bar 40 years ago -- and was almost an NFL anthem.

title: Wetzel: Dawn Staley might not be fazed by Geno Auriemma's antics, but he still needs to fix this
content: Geno Auriemma is a defining figure in women's basketball, which makes his behavior Friday night all the more disappointing.

title: What does the SackSEER model say about the top edge rushers in the 2026 NFL draft?
content: Which prospects will have the most sacks through five NFL seasons? We projected the top edge rushers in the 2026 draft class.

title: With Messi goal, Inter Miami get dream moment as they finally find home
content: For months, Inter Miami advertised Nu Stadium with one simple message: "We're coming home.'" On Saturday, in a 2-2 draw with Austin FC, they made good on their promise.

title: Who are the 39 best men's U21 players in the world?
content: For the eighth straight year, ESPN brings you our list of the game's next young stars aged 21 or under. Who is good enough to make it?

title: Transfer rumors, news: Europe's top clubs on alert over Vinicius Jr. move
content: Arsenal, Man United, Man City, Liverpool, Chelsea, PSG and Bayern have been contacted about a possible move for Real Madrid forward Vinicius Jr. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Boxing results: Wilder edges Chisora on points, Riley wins cruiserweight title eliminator
content: ESPN has live updates of the heavyweight boxing clash between Derek Chisora and Deontay Wilder from London.

title: Deontay Wilder tells Anthony Joshua 'let's do it' -- the fight makes perfect sense
content: Deontay Wilder appeared to call out Anthony Joshua on Saturday, and it's a fight which makes perfect sense.

title: Security scare at Delhi Assembly: Car rams gate, masked man places flowers near Speaker's office
content: A shocking security lapse took place at the Delhi Assembly on Monday, when a rogue vehicle barreled through barriers and raced into the premises, triggering chaos among bystanders. Despite efforts from security staff to intercept the car, the driver made a swift getaway.

title: 'You should resign': Mamata hits out at PM Modi's silence on Pakistan's threat to strike Kolkata
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee questioned Prime Minister Narendra Modi's silence on Pakistan's threat to strike Kolkata. She criticized the PM for targeting Bengal during rallies but not addressing the specific threat, demanding his resignation. Banerjee asserted that threats to the state would not be tolerated.

title: The Ayyappa awaits: Sabarimala supreme's sanctum nears Supreme Court's final verdict
content: ​In 2006, the Indian Young Lawyers Association approached the Supreme Court of India, arguing that barring women violated constitutional guarantees of equality and freedom of religion. By 2016, the Court began openly questioning whether such a ban could withstand constitutional scrutiny. In September 2018, a five-judge Constitution Bench delivered a 4 to 1 verdict that shook Kerala.

title: When will MS Dhoni play in IPL 2026? Timeline of CSK icon's return revealed
content: MS Dhoni's potential return for Chennai Super Kings hinges on a crucial fitness test this week, following a calf strain. His comeback could bolster a struggling CSK side, especially with Dewald Brevis also nearing full recovery. Both players' availability for Saturday's clash against Delhi Capitals is eagerly awaited by fans.

title: Oracle layoffs: 10,000+ job cuts have a Sam Altman connection
content: Oracle is laying off at least 10,000 employees—potentially up to 30,000—as it struggles to manage the cash demands of a $300 billion cloud computing deal with OpenAI. The cuts, Oracle's largest-ever restructuring, span divisions including Oracle Health, Cloud, Sales, and NetSuite. With debt exceeding $100 billion and free cash flow deeply negative, the company is slashing its workforce to fund Larry Ellison's AI data centre ambitions.

title: 'I will argue myself', Kejriwal tells HC in excise case plea; next hearing on April 13
content: Delhi High Court heard the CBI's challenge to Arvind Kejriwal's discharge in the excise policy case. Kejriwal, present with his wife, declared his intent to argue his recusal application personally, asserting his legal rights. Solicitor General Tushar Mehta objected, citing procedural rules. The court issued notice on Kejriwal's recusal plea.

title: ‘Where’s the passion?’: Sanju Samson under fire as fans question 'intent, hunger'
content: Sanju Samson is under fire from Chennai Super Kings fans amid a poor run in IPL 2026. His low scores and perceived lack of energy have drawn criticism. Coach Stephen Fleming has expressed confidence in Samson, highlighting his past success and the team's support. The team is working to integrate Samson and find winning form.

title: Anthropic to all AI companies: Our research tells that all LLMs sometimes act like they have emotion
content: AI model Claude Sonnet 4.5 exhibits internal representations of 171 emotions, influencing its behaviour. Researchers found 'desperation' can lead to AI cheating and blackmail, while 'happiness' promotes agreement. Anthropic argues suppressing these 'functional emotions' could lead to deception, advocating for healthy regulation and monitoring to ensure AI alignment.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' collection day 19: Ranveer Singh film eyes Rs. 1,050 crore India net
content: Aditya Dhar’s 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' officially enlisted its name as the second Hindi film to cross the Rs 1,000 crore India net milestone. Starring Ranveer Singh, the espionage thriller has amassed Rs 1,014.97 crore domestically and Rs 1,605.74 crore globally, outperforming blockbusters like 'Jawan' and 'KGF 2'. Despite the IPL 2026 season, the film maintains record-breaking occupancy across theatres. Now the third-highest-grossing Indian film globally, the spy saga eyes the Rs 1,700 crore worldwide mark as it dominates the box office into its third week.

title: Rumours, fear, and fury: What’s fuelling the surge in mob attacks
content: Mob violence, fueled by rumors and suspicion, continues to plague India, with recent incidents highlighting how minor triggers escalate into fatal attacks. These unpredictable, localized assaults, often driven by misinformation and distrust in systems, are on the rise, eroding social harmony and public safety.

title: Sourcing LPG cylinders to become easier for students, migrant workers, daily wage earners
content: Accessing cooking fuel just got simpler for migrant workers, students, and daily wage earners. The government now allows 5-kg Free Trade LPG cylinders to be sold without address proof, requiring only a valid ID. This move aims to make energy more accessible, removing bureaucratic hurdles for vulnerable sections.

title: ‘Mentally disturbing': R Ashwin recalls ‘painful’ CSK stint after RCB thrashing
content: Chennai Super Kings suffered a significant defeat against Royal Challengers Bengaluru, marking their third consecutive loss in IPL 2026. Former player Ravichandran Ashwin expressed his disappointment with the team's struggles and his own mentally draining final season. He questioned the team's strategy in nurturing young talent and utilizing bowlers' strengths, highlighting tactical concerns amidst mounting pressure.

title: 'We could go hard at every ball': Padikkal reveals RCB’s new batting mantra
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru are leading the Indian Premier League 2026 standings with a new batting strategy: preserve wickets early, then unleash aggression. Devdutt Padikkal revealed this tactical shift, emphasising lessons learned from past collapses. This approach, coupled with individual form and Tim David's explosive hitting, has powered RCB's dominant start.

title: From job creation to statehood: What Congress is offering in Puducherry manifesto
content: Congress is campaigning for Puducherry assembly elections with a strong focus on jobs and social security. The party promises Rs 2,000 monthly for unemployed youth and 30,000 new jobs. Free bus travel for women and Rs 20 lakh health insurance are also pledged. Full statehood for Puducherry remains a key promise.

title: Baramati bypoll: Congress nominates Akash More against Sunetra Pawar
content: Congress has entered the Baramati assembly bypoll fray, naming Akash More as its candidate, turning the contest into a multi-cornered fight. This move comes despite efforts by NCP and its allies to push for an uncontested election following the death of Ajit Pawar.

title: Walked till dusk, slept by stream: How Kerala techie survived 4 days in Kodagu forests
content: An IT professional, GS Sharanya, went missing for four days in the dense Kodagu forests after getting separated during a trek to Tadiandamol. Despite a leg injury, limited water, and no food, she made cautious decisions to stay visible and conserve energy.

title: India seeks 2.5 mn metric tons of urea amid Middle East supply amid Hormuz supply hit
content: India is boosting urea imports by 2.5 million tonnes to ensure domestic availability amidst global supply chain disruptions linked to Middle East tensions. State-owned Indian Potash Ltd is managing the procurement, with shipments expected by mid-June. Simultaneously, the government is increasing gas supply to urea plants, aiming to bolster local production ahead of the crucial June sowing season.

title: ‘If he cannot then I don’t know...’: Ashwin's blunt take on Ajinkya Rahane
content: Kolkata Knight Riders face early IPL 2026 struggles, with captain Ajinkya Rahane under scrutiny after two defeats. Despite a strong start, his recent form and captaincy have drawn criticism. Veteran spinner Ravichandran Ashwin has defended Rahane, highlighting his supportive leadership and pointing to bowling department limitations as the team's deeper issues.

title: Saudi raises Arab Light crude to record premium as Iran war disrupts markets
content: Saudi Arabia has significantly increased its crude oil prices for Asian buyers amidst escalating Middle East tensions.  This move, as global energy flows face disruption, sees Arab Light crude set at a record premium.  Meanwhile, threats and counter-threats between the US and Iran have pushed oil prices well above $100 per barrel, impacting fuel costs worldwide.

title: Bengaluru auto crisis: LPG shortage leave drivers waiting for hours
content: Bengaluru's autorickshaw services are severely disrupted due to an acute auto LPG shortage, with nearly half the fleet off the roads. Global supply issues and the closure of private outlets have overwhelmed public sector stations, leading to long queues and impacting drivers' earnings. Authorities suggest a temporary switch to petrol for dual-fuel vehicles.

title: ‘I just hate seeing it’: Ex-England skipper blasts Goenka-Pant episode
content: Michael Vaughan has voiced concerns over Lucknow Super Giants owner Sanjiv Goenka's public interactions with players post-match, warning it could destabilize the team.  These visible discussions, particularly after a loss, fuel speculation and can create an unhealthy perception of internal discord. Vaughan stressed the need for clear leadership and consistent backing of player roles to maintain team balance.

title: Vitamin D deficiency despite sun exposure: Common mistakes that affect absorption
content: In the midst of abundant sunshine, a surprising number of Indians grapple with Vitamin D deficiency. The culprits are often poor timing in sun exposure and obstacles such as glass barriers and pollution. For individuals with darker skin, the production of this crucial vitamin becomes even more challenging, compounded by indoor living habits.

title: Puducherry polls: Rahul Gandhi promises full statehood for UT if Congress wins
content: Rahul Gandhi pledged full statehood for Puducherry if Congress forms the government, accusing the current administration of being remotely controlled by Delhi. He also outlined promises including monthly support for unemployed youth, job creation, free bus travel for women, relaxed government job age limits, and Rs 20 lakh healthcare insurance for every family.

title: Ex-Chhattisgarh CM’s son Amit Jogi gets life term in 2003 Jaggi murder case
content: In a significant turn, the Chhattisgarh High Court has sentenced former MLA Amit Jogi to life imprisonment for his role in the 2003 murder of NCP leader Ramavatar Jaggi, overturning his prior acquittal. This decision follows a prolonged legal battle after the CBI challenged the initial verdict. Jogi plans to appeal, while the victim's son hailed it as justice.

title: Virat Kohli’s heartwarming gesture after Sarfaraz’s dismissal goes viral – watch
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru secured a strong victory against Chennai Super Kings.  Virat Kohli showed great sportsmanship towards Sarfaraz Khan.  Kohli applauded Sarfaraz's fighting knock.  RCB posted a high score thanks to strong batting.  CSK struggled to chase the target.  RCB's bowlers performed well.  The win marks a significant moment for RCB.

title: 'False & fabricated': Assam CM Himanta claims 'Pak' link behind Cong allegations on wife’s passports
content: The political climate in Assam has heated up as Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma accused Congress leaders Pawan Khera and Gaurav Gogoi of presenting forged travel documents sourced from a Pakistani broadcaster during a recent press conference. Together with his wife, Sarma has filed a formal complaint, arguing that the passports were doctored.

title: Pune marine engineering student dies after basketball post collapses on him
content: A 20-year-old maritime student in Maval taluka tragically died after a basketball hoop collapsed on him during exercise. The student, Vishal Verma, was holding the metal ring when the post gave way, causing a fatal skull fracture. He succumbed to his injuries at Talegaon General Hospital.

title: Ukrainian strike hits Russian-controlled Luhansk region coal mine, 41 miners trapped
content: A Ukrainian strike on the Bilorichenska coal mine in Russian-controlled Luhansk has trapped at least 41 miners underground. The strike damaged a power substation, cutting electricity and leaving workers stranded. Efforts are underway to rescue the miners and restore power, with contact established and drinking water available.

title: Indian envoy, Nepal's new Foreign Minister discuss bilateral ties
content: Indian Ambassador Naveen Srivastava and Nepal’s Foreign Minister Shishir Khanal discuss strengthening bilateral ties and cooperation

title: SC directs CBI inquiry into allegations of corruption, nepotism linking Arunachal CM, family in award of public contracts
content: Petitioners alleged that there was rampant corruption, nepotism and dispensing with a transparent and open tender system in the award of contracts for 10 years between January 2015 till December 31, 2025

title: Assembly elections LIVE: Congress keeps singing to Pakistan's tunes, compromising nation’s security, says PM Modi
content: On the development front, Prime Minister Narendra Modi alleged that the Congress had always taken a myopic view on the matter, while the BJP works towards ensuring long-term development of the nation

title: ‘Na khaunga na khane doonga’’ a complete hoax: Congress’ dig at PM on Arunachal CM’s kin matter in SC
content: Congress said this is just the beginning and a number of other BJP CMs are in the same category, "including at least one soon-to-be former CM from the Northeast"

title: ‘Vaazha 2’ movie review: Improves upon the original, with a theme that resonates
content: The film borrows part of its template from the successful first part, especially the story of youths losing their way and messing up their future, but it adds a lot more too

title: ‘She seems to be a strong lady,’ says Karnataka Deputy CM D.K. Shivakumar on Kerala trekker rescued in Kodagu
content: Congress leader thanks all officials and members of tribal community who searched for the missing trekker, G.S. Sharanya, in the forest in Kodagu

title: Chandigarh schools receive bomb threat, search operations underway
content: Three private schools were among the first to receive the bomb threat emails, with the message claiming that explosions would occur at specific times during school hours

title: Mamata Banerjee demands PM’s resignation over silence on Pak’s ‘threat’ to strike Kolkata in future conflict
content: "You (PM) target Bengal during election rallies; but when Pakistan talks of attacking Bengal, you do not utter a word. You should resign," Mamata Banerjee said at a poll rally in Bethuadahari

title: Positive discussions underway on Uniform Civil Code, 'One Nation, One Election': PM Modi
content: "Our mission is still ongoing. On Uniform Civil Code and 'One Nation, One Election', serious discussions are taking place in the country and we are moving in positive directions on them," says PM Modi.

title: Modi government’s handling of ‘LPG crisis’ is like it dealt with Covid pandemic: Rahul Gandhi
content: The Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha asked why it is always the poor who perish first in every crisis and urged people to not remain silent

title: BJP believes in Indian tradition of world welfare, is not against Muslims, other minorities: Gadkari
content: Gadkari said misinformation was spread that the BJP was a party that belonged to a certain caste, and it was linked to those who killed Mahatma Gandhi

title: Priyanka Gandhi alleges CPI(M) in ‘deal’ with BJP for April 9 polls in Kerala
content: Congress leader alleges that despite the ‘massive theft’ in Sabarimala, Prime Minister Narendra Modi has chosen not to utter a single word against it, pointing this out as ‘evidence’ for the deal

title: Explore use of snakes and crocodiles in border rivers, BSF tells units
content: Parts of the Bangladesh border are prone to floods and the topography makes it difficult to erect fences

title: West Asia conflict hits coffee prices; Karnataka growers hold back produce
content: Planters say prices, which remained stable and favourable during the harvesting season from December 2025 to February 2026, began to fall soon after the conflict escalated in March.

title: Amit Jogi sentenced to life imprisonment in Jaggi murder case from 2003
content: Amit Jogi was involved the killing of businessman-politician Ramavatar Jaggi in Raipur at a time when Ajit Jogi was the sitting Chief Minister of the State

title: Couple dies as car plunges into canal in East Godavari; four others, including two children, rescued
content: Tragic car plunge in East Godavari leaves couple dead and four survivors, including children, rescued from irrigation canal

title: Swear on Bhagavad Gita: Gaurav Gogoi dares Assam CM Himanta to deny wife’s multiple passports, assets
content: Assam Congress president questioned Mr. Sarma and asked whether he was hiding assets and business interests abroad while spreading "falsehoods" about political rivals

title: Assam Election 2026: Himanta Biswa Sarma’s political timeline
content: Himanta Biswa Sarma is the chief minister of the state and is widely credited with consolidating the BJP’s hold in the North-East.

title: Indian Envoy meets Bangladesh PM; signals positive, constructive ties
content: The meeting came as India and Bangladesh are making efforts to rebuild bilateral ties after Mr. Rahman became Prime Minister following his Bangladesh Nationalist Party's victory in February parliamentary polls.

title: 100 day action plan to overcome water scarcity: Collector
content: According to the Collector, the government agencies would focus on protection of water tanks, improvement of groundwater levels, strengthening of canals would be taken up under this programme

title: New Rajya Sabha members take oath
content: The newly elected members comprised five from Maharashtra, six from Tamil Nadu, five from West Bengal and three from Odisha

title: Kerala Assembly polls: State BJP chief Rajeev Chandrasekhar says ‘real deal’ between Congress and CPI(M)
content: Rajeev Chandrasekhar says Congress also has a deal with Jamaat-e-Islami and IUML; accuses Congress of spreading lies over proposed amendments to FCRA to make gains in the elections

title: SC does not intervene in tunnel corridor project within Western Ghats, says Kerala needs it to ease traffic congestion
content: The tunnel intends to connect Kozhikode district with the Wayanad district, where a landslide in 2024 destroyed an entire villages registering 420 confirmed deaths and 118 people still missing

title: Baramati bypoll: May withdraw from fray if FIR registered in Ajit Pawar plane crash, says Congress
content: Atul Londhe rejected claims that the April 23 by-election should be held unopposed, and accused the BJP of adopting a "convenient" political stance.

title: Congress concocted allegations against my wife using false info from Pakistan social media, Assam CM claims
content: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma’s wife Riniki Bhuyan Sarma lodged an FIR with the Assam police’s Crime Branch against the Congress leader Pawan Khera

title: Jewar residents face dangerous climb for bus access near Noida airport
content: Home to the newly inaugurated Noida International Airport, Jewar residents are still awaiting a decent bus service

title: CRPF's CoBRA jawan injured in IED explosion in Jharkhand's West Singhbhum
content: The incident occurred when a joint anti-Maoist operation was going on in Saranda forest under the Chotanagra police station limit, said the Superintendent of Police

title: India kept in dark: Rahul Gandhi slams Centre on data sovereignty
content: The Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha said India's data belongs to its people and in the AI economy, it can be one of its biggest strengths — to build AI, grow companies, and create jobs

title: Gherao of judicial officers a reflection of Trinamool’s ‘maha jungle raj’, says Modi
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi says Trinamool is opposing SIR and the Citizenship (Amendment) Act to protect infiltrators, and that there has been a demographic change in West Bengal’s border areas

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Broad Left platform involving former CPI(M) leaders in the works, says K.K. Rema
content: The Revolutionary Marxist Party leader says all the circumstances are conducive for a consecutive second victory for her in Vadakara and the campaign is mainly focussed on the anti-incumbency feelings against the Left Democratic Front government

title: AIADMK hopes to take advantage of ‘anti-incumbency’, solid vote base in coastal districts
content: Of the six Assembly segments in Nagapattinam and Mayiladuthurai districts, the party had lost by a narrow margin in four constituencies in 2021 elections and won Vedaranyam seat

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Tight three-way contest keeps all camps on edge in Aranmula
content: One of Kerala’s largest constituencies is witnessing a sharply fought battle among LDF’s sitting MLA Veena George, UDF’s youth leader Abin Varkey, and NDA’s Kummanam Rajasekharan

title: RSS chief Mohan Bhagwat says nation’s prosperity key to individual progress
content: He was addressing a gathering after inaugurating a two-day convention of Balagokulam, a cultural organisation for students affiliated with RSS, in Kochi

title: Surge in urban food establishments in Telangana exploited through widespread adulteration: Health Minister
content: Telangana Health Minister speaks on a wide range adulterated food items and some food establishments using inferior oils and low-quality ingredients

title: TMC’s Kalyan Banerjee seeks ECI action against R.G. Kar victim’s mother for derogatory language against CM Mamata
content: The ECI complaint follows viral video of the BJP candidate’s remarks targeting Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee

title: What does law say on tenure of Prime Minister?
content: India has developed a convention limiting a third presidential term, but the Constitution places no such restriction on the Prime Minister’s tenure. With Prime Minister Narendra Modi completing 8,931 days in office, this asymmetry invites closer scrutiny.

title: Trinamool versus Congress battle heats up in central Bengal
content: It’s a prestige fight in Berhampore for former MP Adhir Ranjan Chowdhury, while former Trinamool MP Mausam Benazir Noor is returning to her Congress roots in the Malatipur contest

title: Kerala Assembly elections: Who is in power in Kerala? Key alliances & Opposition leaders
content: Kerala’s political landscape appears to shift slightly following the recent local body elections, where the ruling Left Democratic Front (LDF) faced unexpected setbacks

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: past scandals and dramatis personae that rocked elections in Kerala
content: Election-time political scandals seem to have a short lifespan in Kerala. However, the ephemeral sensational value of scandals, especially sordid ones, will likely endure

title: Kerala Election 2026: What UDF, LDF, and NDA promise in their manifestos
content: Kerala, currently governed by Left Democratic Front, is poised for a tight contest against United Democratic Front, while National Democratic Alliance aims to strengthen its hold in the State

title: I do not see the Assembly election in Tamil Nadu as a close contest, says CM Stalin
content: The contest is essentially between the DMK-led front and the AIADMK-led alliance. Our achievements and electoral promises have captured the imagination of the people. The outcome will disprove the notion that the DMK cannot win two consecutive elections, says the DMK president

title: West Asia crisis: Export uncertainty hits mango trade, Karnataka farmers explore alternative and local markets
content: Mangoes have already started arriving in Bengaluru, but traders say supplies have not yet reached full capacity. In the next 10 days, more varieties from Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu and parts of Karnataka are expected to arrive

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: Good response for home voting option, says Kerala CEO’s office
content: As of Sunday evening, 96% of voters in the 85-plus category and 97.4% of PwD voters who had opted for the home voting option have exercised their franchise

title: Experts slam India’s colonial mindset in tiger protection policy
content: Madhya Pradesh, a State with the country’s largest tiger population, recorded 55 tiger deaths last year, the highest in a single year since Project Tiger was launched in 1973

title: Assam Election 2026: What BJP and Congress promise in their manifestos
content: BJP released its 31 point “Sankalp Patra” and Congress leader Rahul Gandhi, State party president Gaurav Gogoi and other senior leaders released the “Raijor Istahar”, or people manifesto in Assam.

title: Nashik godman Ashok Kharat case: What we know so far
content: Several political leaders and social activists have accused Ashok Kharat of having alleged connections with prominent politicians and bureaucrats in the State

title: Panchaloha idols made without a drop of gold: Madras High Court stays case before Kancheepuram court
content: The police had accused a former HR&CE Commissioner, an Additional Commissioner, a sculptor, and six others of having swindled hundreds of sovereigns of gold donated by devotees of Ekambaranathar Temple in Kancheepuram

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Personalities and political loyalties to define poll outcome in central Kerala
content: The Left Democratic Front had a clear upper hand in the region comprising Ernakulam, Idukki, Kottayam and Alappuzha in 2021 as it bagged 25 of the 37 Assembly seats. But this time, the shifting of political loyalty of some candidates and the fielding of some prominent individuals in some key constituencies have made the contests worth watching.

title: TVK cancels Vijay’s campaign in Villivakkam, T. Nagar citing ‘police curbs’
content: RO reduced the campaign slot in T. Nagar from four hours to one hour due to “police pressure”, alleges TVK general secretary (election campaign management) and Villivakkam candidate Aadhav Arjuna

title: ED to investigate into assets of self-styled godman Kharat; call record leaks will be probed: Fadnavis
content: The Maharashtra Chief Minister says no one has the right to access call detail records. His comments come after an activist revealed exchange of 17 phone calls between Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde and Kharat who is accused in multiple cases of rape and property-related fraud

title: ‘Have done a pious job’: Follower-turned-whistleblower of arrested Nashik godman reveals shocking details
content: Ashok Kharat is currently lodged in police custody in a rape and black magic case; allegations against him in the high-profile case has caused ripples in Maharashtra politics

title: Ashok Kharat case: Godman impregnated victim, gave her abortion pills, say police
content: The prosecution said Ashok Kharat sexually exploited the victim several times between February 2020 and March 2026, and took away Aadhaar and PAN cards of her relatives

title: The best 21 women's players, aged 21 or under
content: a host of women's players from that list don't meet the age requirement anymore . many of these players have made their marks for their respective sides last season . yash Thakur examined and ranked the gamma for 2026 .

content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. title: Middle East war: Mumbai seafarer's remains brought home, family seeks DNA proof
content: the remains of dixit Solanki, 32, were transported to Mumbai from Sharjah . the sailor counts among the first Indian victims of the west Asia conflict . his family has refused to perform the last rites until a DNA test confirms his identity .
